issue_title: Upgrade with Mysql and migration-strategy=manual when db user has non alter table permissions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We are trying to upgrade form 26.2 and getting the following error:

liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ALTER command denied to user 'keycloak'@'localhost' for table 'databasechangelog' [Failed SQL: (1142) ALTER TABLE rhbak.databasechangelog ADD PRIMARY KEY (ID, AUTHOR, FILENAME)]

Our Keycloak MySQL user does not have ALTER TABLE permissions and we are using the Manual Keycloak Migration Strategy to generate the keycloak-database-update.sql script. The script is then executed manually using a high-privilege database user.




### Version

26.4

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Add alter table sentence to keycloak-database-update.sql.

### Actual behavior

Tries to update table with low privileges user.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a user in mysql with low privileges and try to upgrade from 26.2

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add PK creation for databasechangelog in MySQL to keycloak-database-update.sql when manual migration is used.

Closes #44349

### Area

### Describe the bug

In Keycloak 26.4.0 to 26.4.5, the Fine-Grained Admin Permissions (FGAP) code path executes even when FGAP is disabled at both realm and client levels. This causes service accounts with the create-realm role to receive 403 Forbidden errors when attempting to assign realm client roles to users via the Admin REST API.

This is a regression from 26.3.3, which worked correctly.

#### Additional Context

This affects both direct client role assignments and composite roles containing client roles
The same operation works successfully via the Keycloak Admin Console UI when assigned admin role to master realm client.
This appears to be a regression introduced between 26.3.x and 26.4.x

This blocks automation tools from managing realm-management role assignments using service accounts, forcing manual intervention through the Admin Console.

issue_title: Unlocalized date format in (email messages)
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area translations ### Describe the bug When using date parameters in the `msg` formatter, they are not formatted according to the user locale. Freemarker wraps the Java `Date` object in [`SimpleData`](https://freemarker.apache.org/docs/api/freemarker/template/SimpleDate.html) object and the `MessageFormatterMethod` considers it a `SimpleScalar`, which is converted to a `String`. see: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/beans/MessageFormatterMethod.java#L72-L73 While Freemarker would use the locale to format a date, this is no longer possible with the stringified date. A simple fix could look like: ``` if (item instanceof SimpleDate) { item = ((SimpleDate) item).getAsDate(); } else if (item instanceof SimpleScalar) { item = ((SimpleScalar) item).getAsString(); } ``` ### Version 26.4.2 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Emails contain a correctly formatted date according the users locale. In the example case, the format should be something like `21.11.25, 09:46`. ### Actual behavior Receiving a mail containing a date, it is not correctly formatted to the users locale. An example with a German locale setting: ### How to Reproduce? - Activate "email" event listener in realm settings -> Events - Configure realm email settings so you can receive emails - Configure (additionally) German as a supported realm language - Configure a user with an email address which you can access - Login as a realm user into account console - Set user locale to German - Update password ("Anmeldung" -> "Aktualisieren") - An email was sent to the user which contains the formatted date, but with a non-German format ### Anything else? I am planning to provide a pull request for this.
commit_message:
fix: handle localized date formatting in message format

Closes #44377

Signed-off-by: Theresa Henze <theresa.henze@bare.id>

issue_title: Node.js admin client does not refresh tokens
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client-js

### Describe the bug

The Node.js admin client obtain an initial access token, but does not check or refresh this token later. This will cause issues when using the admin client for a longer period of time.

As an example this causes the Account E2E tests to be flaky as they sometimes can take longer to execute than the validity of the token. Especially in private repositories.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Admin client automatically refreshes the access token if it is close to expiring. A default of 5 seconds minimum validity would be sensible.

### Actual behavior

The admin client never refreshes the access token. A custom token provider can be used to do this, but that is  not easy to implement.

### How to Reproduce?

Run Account E2E tests in a private repository, or simple call `auth` then wait for more than 5 minutes before calling any operations through the admin endpoints.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
added ability to refresh token when within time (#45789)

fixes: #44379

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: Delete user step in workflows is not removing other scheduled steps the user might have
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If a user has multiple scheduled steps, and one of the workflows runs the `delete-user` step, the user is removed from the local DB but the other scheduled steps/workflows remain in the database.

This happens because the step is not running the deletion through `UserManager`, which is necessary to trigger the event that is used to ensure the workflow state table is cleaned up as well.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a workflow removes a user through the `delete-user` step, all other scheduled steps associated with the user in the workflow state table should be removed as well.

### Actual behavior

When user is removed by the `delete-user` step, other scheduled steps in the workflow state table are not being removed.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup two workflows with scheduled tasks. Ensure one of them has the `delete-user` step, and that this step runs before the other workflow's scheduled step (can be any step). Once the user is removed, check the state table - the other scheduled step is still there.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure delete step is triggering UserRemovedEvent

Closes #44398

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Workflows are restarting on any event instead of the one that activates it
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When we have a workflow with concurrency setting `cancel-if-running=true` are restarting on any event when they should be restarting only if the event matches the workflow's `on` condition.

Example:
```
name: disable inactive users
enabled: true
on: user-logged-in(account-console)
concurrency:
  cancel-if-running: true
if: "!has-role(admin)"
steps:
- uses: notify-user
  with:
    custom_message: Your account can be disabled due to inactivity
- uses: disable-user
  after: 300
- uses: delete-user
  after: 300
```

This workflow is triggered only when the user logs in to the account-console client. The concurrency setting is used to specify that the workflow should be cancelled/restarted when the same event is received again - i.e. user logs back in to the account-console. However, the current implementation is restarting the workflow on ANY event supported by Workflows.


### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Workflow should only be restarted if the `on` condition is satisfied by the event being processed. Events unrelated to the activation of the workflow should not have an impact on its execution.

### Actual behavior

When a workflow has a concurrency setting, any event is causing the workflow to be restarted.

### How to Reproduce?

Create the workflow in the description. Then log into the account console with any non-admin user. The workflow will run, executing the notify step immediately, and then scheduling the second task (disable user). Now make the same user join a group, which will fire a group membership added event. Notice that the workflow is restarted - the first step is run again, and the second step is re-scheduled.

The workflow should only restart when the users logs into the account again. Any other event should be discarded.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure workflow is only restarted on events that match the activation condition

Closes #44399

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Removing a user's group is not activating workflows that use the user-group-membership-removed event
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If we define a workflow that activates on the `user-group-membership-removed` event, the workflow is never activated. This happens because Keycloak's  `GroupMemberLeaveEvent` doesn't have the user who left the group, which causes the `WorkflowEventListener` to fail to build the corresponding workflow event as the resource id (in this case the user id) is required.

We need to ensure the `GroupMemberLeaveEvent` has a reference to the user whose role was revoked so that the corresponding workflow event can be built.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)` should activate when the user leaves the group.

### Actual behavior

Workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)` is not activating when the user leaves the group.

### How to Reproduce?

Create any workflow with `on: user-group-membership-removed(somegroup)`. Add the user as a member of the referenced group. Then remove the user from the group - the workflow should activate but it doesn't.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure GroupMemberLeaveEvent has a reference to the user leaving the group

Closes #44400

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: DCR endpoint ignores client's requested token_endpoint_auth_method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

## Summary

Keycloak's Dynamic Client Registration (DCR) endpoint ignores the client's requested `token_endpoint_auth_method` and always returns `client_secret_basic`, even when clients explicitly request `client_secret_post`.

## Impact

This breaks compatibility with OAuth clients that require `client_secret_post`, including:
- Model Context Protocol (MCP) clients (per RFC 9110 requirement)
- Clients that cannot support HTTP Basic authentication
- Environments with security policies requiring POST-based authentication

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Per [RFC 7591 Section 2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591#section-2), the `token_endpoint_auth_method` field indicates authentication method for the token endpoint requested by the client. Since `client_secret_post` is a standard method supported by Keycloak, the server should honor this request.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak always returns `client_secret_basic` regardless of the client's request, effectively ignoring the parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

### Setup Keycloak

Use the preconfigured test realm from FastMCP (includes DCR enabled and trusted hosts configured):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp.git
cd fastmcp
git checkout feat/keycloak-auth-provider
cd examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak
./start-keycloak.sh  # Imports realm with DCR enabled
```

Setup details: [README.md](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/README.md)

### Test with cURL

```bash
# DCR request explicitly requesting client_secret_post
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/realms/fastmcp/clients-registrations/openid-connect \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "client_name": "Test Client",
    "redirect_uris": ["http://localhost:8000/callback"],
    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_post"
  }'
```

**Expected:** Response includes `"token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_post"`
**Actual:** Response includes `"token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic"`

## Test Resources

Complete reproduction environment available in FastMCP repo (branch `feat/keycloak-auth-provider`):
- [realm-fastmcp.json](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/realm-fastmcp.json) - Test realm configuration
- [README.md](https://github.com/stephaneberle9/fastmcp/blob/feat/keycloak-auth-provider/examples/auth/keycloak_auth/keycloak/README.md) - Setup instructions

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
DCR endpoint ignores client's requested token_endpoint_auth_method in case it is client_secret_post
closes #44403

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Security issue with Organization feature exposes and fills the account name automatically in user/password form
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Hi,

This is a security issue using the Organization feature.

The issue is that if a user enters its email address, then the users account name will be prefilled in the username/password form, as the entered email address is translated into an account name.
So one can document all the accounts by just knowing the email-address.

Recommendation to fix this issue:
Dont visualize any data in the username/password form.

In our case the users account name (username) is sensitive; so please don't expose any other data then the user enters.

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Dont expose any other data then the user enters.

### Actual behavior

The users username is presented / exposed instead of what has been entered from the user.

### How to Reproduce?

Enable Organization feature. 
Enter an email address
The users account name is presented and prefilled in the username/password form

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Organization feature exposes and fills the account name automatically in user/password form

Closes #44417

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>

issue_title: findClientSessionsClientIds performance issue
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

I have recently observed, that Read IOPS of my Keycloak clusters database occasionally spikes to very high levels.

Debugging showed, that the named query `findClientSessionsClientIds` is responsible for these spikes.

An `explain` of the query verified this - `lastSessionRefresh 0` is used as a dummy value:

    keycloak=> explain analyze select pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER,count(*) from public.OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION pcse1_0 join public.OFFLINE_USER_SESSION puse1_0 on pcse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID=puse1_0.USER_SESSION_ID and puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG=pcse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG where puse1_0.OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and puse1_0.REALM_ID='Customers' and puse1_0.LAST_SESSION_REFRESH>=0 group by pcse1_0.CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.EXTERNAL_CLIENT_ID,pcse1_0.CLIENT_STORAGE_PROVIDER;
                                                                                           QUERY PLAN
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     Finalize GroupAggregate  (cost=741482.34..741488.14 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9226.410..9323.312 rows=6 loops=1)
       Group Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
       ->  Gather Merge  (cost=741482.34..741487.48 rows=44 width=57) (actual time=9226.395..9323.296 rows=18 loops=1)
             Workers Planned: 2
             Workers Launched: 2
             ->  Sort  (cost=740482.32..740482.37 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9196.666..9196.951 rows=6 loops=3)
                   Sort Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
                   Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   Worker 0:  Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   Worker 1:  Sort Method: quicksort  Memory: 25kB
                   ->  Partial HashAggregate  (cost=740481.61..740481.83 rows=22 width=57) (actual time=9196.630..9196.916 rows=6 loops=3)
                         Group Key: pcse1_0.client_id, pcse1_0.external_client_id, pcse1_0.client_storage_provider
                         Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         Worker 0:  Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         Worker 1:  Batches: 1  Memory Usage: 24kB
                         ->  Parallel Hash Join  (cost=336131.55..727419.06 rows=1306255 width=49) (actual time=4632.833..8678.519 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                               Hash Cond: ((pcse1_0.user_session_id)::text = (puse1_0.user_session_id)::text)
                               ->  Parallel Seq Scan on offline_client_session pcse1_0  (cost=0.00..337206.07 rows=1435597 width=88) (actual time=0.028..1324.107 rows=1154159 loops=3)
                                     Filter: ((offline_flag)::text = '1'::text)
                                     Rows Removed by Filter: 26811
                               ->  Parallel Hash  (cost=307384.82..307384.82 rows=1415178 width=39) (actual time=1824.144..1824.145 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                                     Buckets: 131072  Batches: 64  Memory Usage: 4896kB
                                     ->  Parallel Seq Scan on offline_user_session puse1_0  (cost=0.00..307384.82 rows=1415178 width=39) (actual time=0.010..1250.316 rows=1151082 loops=3)
                                           Filter: ((last_session_refresh >= 0) AND ((offline_flag)::text = '1'::text) AND ((realm_id)::text = 'Customers'::text))
                                           Rows Removed by Filter: 94081
     Planning Time: 40.232 ms
     Execution Time: 9323.381 ms
    (27 rows)


`offline_client_session` has a partially fitting index: `"constraint_offl_cl_ses_pk3" PRIMARY KEY, btree (user_session_id, client_id, client_storage_provider, external_client_id, offline_flag)`

`offline_user_session` has a perfect fitting index: `"idx_offline_uss_by_last_session_refresh" btree (realm_id, offline_flag, last_session_refresh)`

But Postgres decides against their use. I've tried adding a better fitting index on `offline_client_session`, but it actually made it worse.

Do you see optimization potential or should I scale up the database? The tables are 2500 MB and 2200 MB, so quite large already. 

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

result in < 1 second

### Actual behavior

> 9 seconds

### How to Reproduce?

call `/{realm}/client-session-stats` of a realm with many sessions

Side question: Did this get removed from the UI?

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Intermittent ConcurrentModificationException during SAML initialization causing status code 400 for clients
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When several requests trigger SAML-related initialization concurrently, a ConcurrentModificationException is occasionally thrown from the SAML module. The stack traces lead back to calls made during the creation of ThreadLocal `DocumentBuilder` instances.

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start consistently without intermittent exceptions, even when many threads initialize their ThreadLocal `DocumentBuilder` instances at the same time.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak startup sometimes fails with a `ConcurrentModificationException` originating from the XML parsing components used by the SAML module. When this occurs, the client attempting to authenticate receives an `HTTP 400` response. The issue is intermittent and typically appears only during multi-threaded startup conditions.

### How to Reproduce?

A JUnit test that spawns multiple threads and repeatedly triggers ThreadLocal initialization of the XML `DocumentBuilder` can expose the issue. The test does not fail on every run—the bug is timing-dependent—but repeatedly running the test will at some point expose the bug where the `ConcurrentModificationException` is thrown, demonstrating that the problem exists.

### Anything else?

I will provide a PR with the failing test and a proposed fix.

Fix race condition in SAML DocumentBuilderFactory creation

issue_title: ClassCastException on mixing AddressMapper with ClaimMapper
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

If the Keycloak internal AddressMapper is used in conjunction with a ClaimMapper that tries to add an additional attribute to the address-structure we get a ClassCastException here:

```java
public class OIDCAttributeMapperHelper {

private static void mapClaim(List<String> split, Object attributeValue, Map<String, Object> jsonObject, boolean isMultivalued) {
          ....
          else {
                @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
                Map<String, Object> nested = (Map<String, Object>) jsonObject.get(component);

                if (nested == null) {
                    nested = new HashMap<>();
                    jsonObject.put(component, nested);
                }

                jsonObject = nested;
            }
        }
}
...
}
```

The reason is that the AddessMapper creates an object of `AddressClaimSet` that is added into the token, while the ClaimAttributeMapper is working with a `Map<String, Object>`.

```
2025-11-25 07:54:17,716 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-33) Uncaught server error: java.lang.ClassCastException: class org.keycloak.representations.AddressClaimSet cannot be cast to class java.util.Map (org.keycloak.representations.AddressClaimSet is in unnamed module of loader io.quarkus.bootstrap.runner.RunnerClassLoader @4bec1f0c; java.util.Map is in module java.base of loader 'bootstrap')
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:345)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:317)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.mapClaim(OIDCAttributeMapperHelper.java:278)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.UserAttributeMapper.setClaim(UserAttributeMapper.java:103)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.setClaim(AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.java:159)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.mappers.AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.transformUserInfoToken(AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper.java:80)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$3.applyMapper(TokenManager.java:821)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$3.applyMapper(TokenManager.java:818)
```



### Version

26.4.5 and before

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Both ClaimMappers should work together and create an extended address-object.
In our specific case we want to add a `type`-claim into the `address`-claim:

```json
"address": {
  ...,
  "type": "STRUCTURED"
}
```

### Actual behavior

An exception occurs and we get a 500 internal server error

### How to Reproduce?

see description

issue_title: "Update email" AIA: "Back to Application" URL invokes OIDC callback with missing parameters
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

With the newly-supported `UPDATE_EMAIL` Application-Initiated Action (documented [here](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/26.4.6/server_admin/#_update-email-workflow)), we can trigger the "update email" flow from within our application.

This is very similar to `UPDATE_PASSWORD`. In fact, from the app's POV, there isn't any difference between invoking `UPDATE_EMAIL` and `UPDATE_PASSWORD`: You redirect to the OIDC authorization endpoint with `kc_action` set, Keycloak takes over for a while and guides the user through some process, and in the end, Keycloak redirects back to the app's OIDC callback URL.

However, with `UPDATE_EMAIL`, when the user is finally redirected back to the app after having verified their new email address, all OIDC parameters are missing. The callback endpoint is invoked without any parameters, like `https://my.app/oidc-callback`.

### Version

26.4.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When clicking the link in the "Verify new email" email, and then "Back to Application" on the Keycloak success screen, Keycloak should redirect to the OIDC callback with correct parameters (just like after a normal login).

If the user clicks on the "Verify new email" link when they are not logged in in Keycloak (e.g. they open it in a different browser, or at a later time), then the verification _still succeeds_ (this is correct).
Clicking "Back to Application" should then ideally first go to the Keycloak login screen (without going through the app at all), and only then redirect back to the app with correct OIDC params.

### Actual behavior

After a user confirms their new email address, they are redirected to the app's OIDC callback without any parameters.

The app then likely shows an error to the user, since this scenario is not valid (should never happen) according to the OIDC spec.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable the `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature for a realm with email verification turned on.
2. Create a simple web app using OIDC / Keycloak (all standard).
3. Log in to the app via Keycloak.
4. (optional) Verify that Application-Initiated Actions work in general by adding and invoking an `UPDATE_PASSWORD` AIA. Observe that your OIDC callback endpoint is correctly invoked.
5. Add and invoke an `UPDATE_EMAIL` AIA.
6. Enter a new email address and confirm.
7. Click the verification link in the email you just got.
8. You should see an "Email updated" informational screen by Keycloak. It also contains a link, "Back to Application". Click it.
9. Observe that you are redirected to your OIDC callback _without any parameters_.
commit_message:
Update email AIA: Back to Application URL invokes OIDC callback with missing parameters

Closes #44488

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>

issue_title: The existence of an organization attribute called “id” is not validated
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

If you add the `Organization Membership mapper` to a client with `Add organization attributes` checked and create an attribute in the organization called `id`, the value of the organization id will be lost and replaced by the value of the created attribute.

Relevant code:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/73b27f59303fb3341569615521e3fba744fe740a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/protocol/mappers/oidc/OrganizationMembershipMapper.java#L165-L169

### Version

26.4.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It should validate it somehow and not allow to add it, or create a breaking change to change the JSON format of how it is returned...

The problem is that the organization attributes and the organization id (added from `Add organization attributes`  option from `Organization Membership mapper`) are created at the same level:

```json
{
  "organizations": {
    "organization_1_alias": {
      "id": "organization_1_id",
      "attr1": ["attr1_value"],
      "attr2": ["attr2_value"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Actual behavior

The value of the organization ID is lost and replaced by the created attribute called `id`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an organization.
2. Create a client scope with an `Organization Membership mapper` with `Add organization attributes` checked.
3. Create a client and add that client scope.
4. Go to that client's client scopes, evaluate a user, and see that the claim is mapped and the id value is the id of the organization.
5. Add an attribute called `id` to the organization.
6. Repeat step 4. In this case, the organization id will be lost and the value of the attribute added in the previous step will appear.

### Anything else?

_No response_

The existence of an organization attribute called id is not validated

Closes #44522

issue_title: LDAP group mapper executed multiple times in the same request
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

The LDAP group mapper is being executed multiple times in the same request.

It is a regression introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8430, where the session/request level cache for group mappings does not take into account the possibility of a user not belonging to any group in LDAP.

As a result, this is causing multiple and unnecessary calls to the LDAP server.

### Version

26.4.6

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The LDAP group mapper executed only once when querying group memberships from LDAP in the same request.

### Actual behavior

The LDAP group mapper is being executed multiple times in the same request.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a LDAP provider
* Create a group mapper
* Create a user so that it is federated to LDAP. The user must not have group memberships
* Authenticate as the user

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OID4VCI Credential request with credential_configuration_id allowed even if credential identifier returned in OIDC token response authz details
issue_body:
### Description

The specification mentions this [here](https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#section-8.2) : 

```
credential_configuration_id: REQUIRED if a credential_identifiers parameter was not returned from the
Token Response as part of the authorization_details parameter. It MUST NOT be used otherwise. 
```
However I can see that for example in `OID4VCSdJwtAuthorizationCodeFlowTest.testCompleteFlowWithClaimsValidationAuthorizationCode()` there is OIDC token response with the `credential identifiers` returned, however the credential-request with `credential_configuration_id` is still successful.

Should Keycloak do something to prevent such request from success?

### Note

Not 100% sure if this is responsibility of the credential issuer and if it is possible to prevent this. Creating this issue for tracking purposes. Might be eventually closed if Keycloak is already fine and cannot do much regarding this.

At the same time, it should be carefully reviewed if we need to support credentialRequest without credentialIdentifier (Credential requests only with credentialConfigurationId parameter). It seems those do not require credential-offer object to be present and ATM I am not 100% sure about the security implications of this... Related code for the reference: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ef011ea4d223a29f3ca99989009dc2941b6959f2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oid4vc/issuance/OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java#L763-L768

issue_title: SAML user created with null username when mapped attribute missing
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area saml ### Describe the bug ## Summary When Keycloak brokers authentication to a customer IdP (SAML) and the mapped SAML attribute for username is absent in the response, Keycloak creates a local user with a null username. This leads to unusable user records and session tracking issues, and the record cannot be deleted from the Admin Console due to DB constraints on federated_identity and user_role_mappings. ## Environment - Keycloak version: 26.4.x and older - Area: identity-brokering - IdP protocol: SAML and OIDC - User profile: Default (profile review enabled on first login) ### Version 26.3.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior - Fail user creation: If the mapped username attribute is missing or resolves to null/empty, user creation should fail with a clear error. - Enforce validation: The profile review page should enforce a non-null, non-empty username before allowing acceptance. - Admin consistency: Admin deletion should not be blocked by dangling DB constraints when a record is invalid. ### Actual behavior - Null user created: Keycloak creates a local user with null username. - Unmanageable record: Admin UI cannot delete the user due to DB constraints. - Profile review leak: Profile review shows username as null and allows acceptance, persisting the invalid value. ### How to Reproduce? - Configure SAML IdP: Set up identity brokering to a customer SAML IdP. - Map username attribute: Create an attribute mapper that maps a SAML attribute to username. - Return SAML without attribute: Have the IdP send a valid SAML Response that omits the mapped username attribute. - First login flow: User is redirected back to Keycloak; profile review page shows username as null. - Accept profile: If the user accepts, Keycloak creates the user with null username. - Attempt deletion: Try deleting the user from Admin Console; deletion fails due to constraints referencing federated_identity and user_role_mappings ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Possible data loss when scaling Keycloak StatefulSet down (on Kubernetes)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

When testing Keycloak scaling down capabilities (within https://github.com/defenseunicorns/uds-core/issues/2003), I discovered that the node properly leaves the cluster but it doesn't take ongoing rebalance into account. As a result, Keycloak scales down very rapidly (going from 5 to 1 instance in a matter of second) which can lead to the data loss when caches contain logs of data. 

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When scaling down, during the shutdown hook, Infinispan should block until an ongoing rebalance completes and data segments are reshuffled. 

### Actual behavior

Infinispan seems to properly leave the cluster but doesn't take the ongoing rebalance into consideration.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Get a healthy cluster of 5 nodes up and running.
2. Scale the StatefulSet from 5 to 1
3. Observe the rapid scale down

### Anything else?

I successfully verified that:

* The StatefulSet controller behaves correctly and the deletion order is correct:

```
$ kubectl get events -n keycloak --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | grep keycloak
15m         Normal    SuccessfulDelete               statefulset/keycloak          delete Pod keycloak-4 in StatefulSet keycloak successful
15m         Normal    SuccessfulDelete               statefulset/keycloak          delete Pod keycloak-3 in StatefulSet keycloak successful
15m         Normal    Killing                        pod/keycloak-3                Stopping container keycloak
15m         Normal    SuccessfulDelete               statefulset/keycloak          delete Pod keycloak-2 in StatefulSet keycloak successful
15m         Normal    Killing                        pod/keycloak-2                Stopping container keycloak
15m         Normal    Killing                        pod/keycloak-1                Stopping container keycloak
15m         Normal    SuccessfulDelete               statefulset/keycloak          delete Pod keycloak-1 in StatefulSet keycloak successful
```

* JGroups properly installs new view and then FD Protocols note the member being down. This order of events suggests a proper implementation of the graceful leave, so I guess shutdown hooks work correctly. 

* I checked the code `DefaultInfinispanConnectionProviderFactory#close` code and indeed it seems to call `CacheManager#stop` properly. 

* I don't have the code at hand right now but I think it used to work correctly with the Golang Operator.
commit_message:
Block shutdown if the Infinispan cluster is not stable

If a rebalance is in progress, block the shutdown procedure until it finishes or a timeout is reached.

Closes #44620

issue_title: OID4VCI functionality should be disabled for the realm when "Verified Credentials" switch is disabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

We have switch `Verified Credentials` in the realm settings in the admin console, which is visible when `oid4vci` feature is enabled.

When this is disabled, the OID4VCI functionality should be probably disabled, but this is not the case.

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-03)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When the switch `Verified Credentials` in the realm settings in the admin console is disabled, it can be good if there are not any references to the OID4VCI in the admin console and account console UI and the related providers and endpoints are disabled as well.


### Actual behavior

- The link to the "OpenID4VCI Credential Issuer Metadata" is shown just when switch enabled: OK behaviour
- The tab "Verifiable credentials" in the account console is shown just when the switch is enabled: OK behaviour
- The various endpoints and grants seems to be still enabled even if this switch is disabled: KO
- Various options in the admin console are still visible when the switch is disabled: KO
- It is possible to create client of `OID4VCI` protocol even if the switch is disabled: KO (should be handled both server-side and admin console UI IMO)
- The client scopes of `oid4vci` are still visible in the client scopes tab: KO (But I think they should be visible when assigning/unassigning them for the clients, so it is possible to eventually unassign them from existing clients if already assigned to them)

### How to Reproduce?

See description and other sections for more details

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Credential offer by reference can be triggered multiple times
issue_body:
### Description

When the credential-offer is sent by reference, the wallet is supposed to invoke the credential offer URL, which typically look something like this (the last part of the path is unique "nonce"): https://as.keycloak-fapi.org:8443/realms/test/protocol/oid4vc/credential-offer/WvGzjQdCaW3oJBimFWDBClcsqnXs_nzK2IB3eOaVjm-XxDbnZfC2PZapZYtYSA9hwnhvzuL6ytGiCr2jejGjPw .

This URL can be triggered multiple times right now. This means that also pre-authorized code could be potentially obtained multiple times. The specification does not seem to have anything about this URL needs to be unique in this section https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#name-sending-credential-offer-by- . It has only something about make sure that pre-authorized code should be unique https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#name-replay-prevention .

This issue is to make sure that we either allow only single request for obtain the pre-authorized code from the credential-offer URL for given "nonce" , or we ensure that pre-authorized code is unique to be used in the token-request of pre-authorized grant.

IMO the replay protection should work even if `tx_code` is not used (I consider `tx_code` handling out of scope of this issue).
commit_message:
[OID4VCI] Add replay protection for credential offers by reference (#45558)

closes #44660

issue_title: CredentialEndpoint can be invoked with incorrect access token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Based on the OID4VCI specification, it is mentioned that access-token used in the credential endpoint should be the one, which represents approval of the user related to granted credential. See for example [this section](https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#section-8) or some other sections of the specs.

However Keycloak currently allows to invoke credential-endpoint with the access token different than the one returned from the pre-authorized grant token response. See for example [this test](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/4dd68c03167e6ee144ef8d7acaa2cf3b43326cb7/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/oid4vc/issuance/signing/OID4VCAuthorizationDetailsFlowTestBase.java#L590) .

We should make sure to prevent this from happening. I suppose that the access-token may need to contain the `scope` representing the requested credential, maybe also `credential_id` should be there in some claim, but not sure... This applies for tokens obtained from pre-authorized code as well as authorization_code grants.

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-04)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It is possible to obtain verifiable credential with the different access-token than the one obtained from the pre-authorization grant token response.

### Actual behavior

It should be possible to obtain verifiable credential only with the access-token returned from the token response described in the [chapter 6 of the specs](https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#name-successful-token-response).

### How to Reproduce?

See the description (There is test, which can be used to simulate this).

### Anything else?

It may probably help to make sure that `authorization_details` are stored as a claim in the access-token instead of storing them as a note in userSession. Related issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44961

issue_title: InMemoryCredentialOfferStorage is not cluster-aware
issue_body:
### Description

There is in-memory implementation `InMemoryCredentialOfferStorage`, which works probably in single cluster node. This limitation should be mitigated to make sure that OID4VCI properly works in cluster similarly like any other Keycloak functionality.
commit_message:
Make OID4VC credential offer storage cluster-aware and rename provider

- Replace InMemoryCredentialOfferStorage with DefaultCredentialOfferStorage backed by singleUseObjects (Infinispan)
- Fix expiration handling by converting absolute expiration timestamps to lifespan seconds and skipping already-expired entries
- Rename factory to DefaultCredentialOfferStorageFactory and change provider id to default
- Update SPI service registration and add JavaDoc clarifying cluster/cross-DC behavior

Closes #44674

issue_title: Inconsistent search when using wildcards
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/api ### Describe the bug **User Search (JpaUserProvider)** Has proper escaping and wildcard handling: ```java // Escapes SQL LIKE special characters and converts * to % value = value.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("%", "\\%").replace("_", "\\_"); value = value.replace("*", "%"); if (value.isEmpty() || value.charAt(value.length() - 1) != '%') value += "%"; orPredicates.add(builder.like(from.get(USERNAME), value, ESCAPE_BACKSLASH)); ``` Behavior: - search="*" with exact=false → matches all users (correct) - search="john_" → matches only "john_" literally (underscore escaped) - search="50%" → matches only "50%" literally (percent escaped) **Organization Member Search (JpaOrganizationProvider)** NO escaping or wildcard handling: ```java // Just wraps value with % for LIKE if (exact) { orPredicates.add(builder.equal(from.get(USERNAME), value)); } else { value = "%" + value + "%"; orPredicates.add(builder.like(from.get(USERNAME), value)); } ``` Behavior: - search="*" with exact=true → matches ZERO users (looks for literal "*") - search="*" with exact=false → SQL: LIKE '%*%' → matches only users with asterisk in name - search="john_" → SQL: LIKE '%john_%' → matches "john_", "johna", "johnX" (underscore is wildcard!) - search="50%" → SQL: LIKE '%50%%' → matches "50%", "500", "50abc" (percent is wildcard!) **There is also a note in upgrading guide.** docs/documentation/upgrading/topics/changes/changes-23_0_0.adoc ``` "Starting with this release the only wildcard character is * which works consistently across all providers in all places in the search string. All special characters in a specific provider like % and _ for JPA are escaped." ``` Which implies that the change was done universally, not only to user search. Reality: - Implemented for: JpaUserProvider (users) - Implemented for: JpaIdentityProviderStorageProvider (identity providers) - thanks to Stefan - NOT implemented for: JpaOrganizationProvider (organization members) - NOT implemented for: JpaRealmProvider (groups) - NOT implemented for: Other search providers (edited) This shows that Keycloak is vulnerable to SQL Wildcard Injection in some cases ### Version latest ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior see description ### Actual behavior see description ### How to Reproduce? see description ### Anything else? We should decide how to approach `exact` param ... for user search we take it into an account only when searching by predefined attributes: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/44cf6d68086b716500d1b77a6e4c22e0bf849348[…]n/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java not when using `search` (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/44cf6d68086b716500d1b77a6e4c22e0bf849348[…]n/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java) or `searchQuery` (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/44cf6d68086b716500d1b77a6e4c22e0bf849348[…]n/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java) When using `search` we rely on double quotes, which is also documented: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/docs/documentation/server_admin/topics/users/proc-searching-user.adoc Admin UI does not allow you to specify exact for user search. Some other searches in admin UI provides it, for instance group search: but there we do not support the double quotes Maybe something for KC 27 as that will likely change the behavior of some searches
commit_message:
Inconsistent search when using wildcards

Closes #44678

issue_title: Not able to find key for credential signature if client scope was saved from admin console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Assume following scenario:

- Import some realm with some OID4VCI client scopes
- Go to some client scope (EG. `sd-jwt-credential`) and save it without any changes. Especially leave the option *Signing Key ID* with the value *Use default (Realm's active signing key)* . At this point, the SigningKeyID is saved with empty string value instead of null
- Now try to send credentialRequest for the credential corresponding to this client scope. It fails due the `AbstractCredentialSigner.getKey` currently fallbacks to the realm active key just if signingKey is null .

It seems we should either make sure that signer is saved from admin console with value null or some different placeholder value (EG. `realm_active_key`) or `AbstractCredentialSigner.getKey` allows also empty strings (or the placeholder value if we use this option).

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-05)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

See description for the details.

### Actual behavior

See description for the details.

### How to Reproduce?

See description for the details.

### Anything else?

I am not 100% sure if there is a need for this option at all? Should it perhaps rather always use current realm active key for the specified algorithm similarly like all other Keycloak functionality is doing? Can we perhaps remove it?

Proposal to removing the option of `Signing Key ID` and instead keep only the option for selected signature algorithm (single algorithm like used for example in OIDC clients for stuff like `ID Token signature algorithm`): https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/44765#issuecomment-3647303529 . If we go this approach, it should fix this bug as well.
commit_message:
Not able to find key for credential signature if client scope was saved from admin console
closes #44699
